name: Veggie Scramble with Goat Cheese & Almond Toast
method: Macros (approx) — Dave: 545kcal | 36g protein | 16g fat | 58g carbs. Mel: 405kcal | 24g protein | 12g fat | 44g carbs.
Whisk the eggs with a splash of goat's milk and season.
Sauté the peppers, spinach and spring onion in a little olive oil for 2-3 minutes until soft.
Pour in the eggs and gently scramble until just set, then crumble in the goat's cheese at the last minute.
Toast the almond bread and serve alongside the scramble.

name: Date & Seed Breakfast Bars (Batch Made)
method: Macros (approx) — Dave: 535kcal | 34g protein | 16g fat | 59g carbs. Mel: 395kcal | 23g protein | 12g fat | 45g carbs.
Blend dates with peanut butter until a sticky paste forms.
Stir in buckwheat flakes, mixed seeds and a pinch of cinnamon.

name: Saturday Healthy Full English
method: Macros (approx) — Dave: 555kcal | 38g protein | 17g fat | 55g carbs. Mel: 405kcal | 25g protein | 13g fat | 42g carbs.
Saturday special. Grill the pork sausages and turn occasionally until cooked through.
Fry the eggs in a little olive oil, and grill the tomatoes and mushrooms alongside.
Warm the beans separately.
Toast the almond bread and plate everything together.

name: Tofu Breakfast Scramble with Spinach
method: Macros (approx) — Dave: 530kcal | 35g protein | 15g fat | 58g carbs. Mel: 395kcal | 23g protein | 11g fat | 45g carbs.

Batch-cook: brown the gluten-free chicken sausage meat and divide between a greased muffin tray.
Whisk eggs with chopped peppers and goat's cheese, pour over the sausage.
Bake at 180°C for 18-20 minutes until set.
Store extras in the fridge for up to 3 days.
